title: Rounding the Square Root to the Nearest Integer

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The method will be given an integer n.
statement.outputFormat: Return the integer value of the square root of n rounded to the nearest integer.
statement.notes: This problem is about math functions.

examples.explanation: The square root of 5 is approximately 2.236, rounded down to 2.
The square root of 16 is exactly 4.

hints.text: Use the built-in int function to remove the decimal part of a float

editorial.title: The Solution
editorial.intuition: The solution should take advantage of the built-in sqrt and abs functions.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force solution would be to iterate from 0 to n, and check if the square of the current number is equal to n.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the built-in sqrt function and then cast it to an integer to get the square root rounded down.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This function works because the built-in sqrt function returns a float, and when we cast it to an integer, the decimal part is truncated.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run with the test cases
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use the abs function to handle negative numbers
Using a complex formula instead of the built-in sqrt function
